program: /* # The Declare homepage The page you are reading, written in Declare. The design tokens live once as semantic type components, and the page is a vertical **flow** of auto-sizing sections — no absolute-y. Each section's height falls out of its content, so the page stacks and re-flows on its own at any width. The full-bleed chrome — the blueprint grid, the sticky header, the pointer dot — sits absolutely around the centered column. `{ }` bodies are TypeScript, so colors there are `0x…`; `[ ]` literal slots take `#…`. Every colour on the page has a name in the App's `theme` record — the palette lives there, once, and nowhere else. */ font Sans [ family = "system-ui" ] font Mono [ family = "ui-monospace" ] /* ## Type as tokens Every text style on the page is one of these classes — headings semibold with tracking that scales with the size, eyebrows mono and wide-tracked, lead and body in the muted tone — so the page's whole typography reskins from here. */ class Heading extends Text [ fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.text }, letterSpacing = { -0.02 * this.fontSize }, selectable = true, ] class Eyebrow extends Text [ fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, letterSpacing = 1.6, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, selectable = true, ] // the accent section number class Marker extends Eyebrow [ textColor = { this.theme.accent } ] // footer text in the Declare typeface (App Sans), not the mono eyebrow class FootLabel extends Text [ fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, selectable = true ] class Lead extends Text [ fontSize = 19, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { this.theme.lead }, selectable = true ] class Body extends Text [ fontSize = 17, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { this.theme.muted }, selectable = true ] // an article paragraph (the "Why" view) — HTML flow, so its `"""` source wraps // at the writer's margin and the line breaks collapse away in the rendering class Para extends HTMLText [ fontSize = 18, lineHeight = 1.2, width = { parent.width }, bodyColor = { this.theme.lead }, selectable = true, ] /* ## The header pills A nav link is a padded pill: it fills with surface-2 on hover (a rounded rect behind the text, not just a colour change), and `pressed` deepens the pill and lights the label — the tap feedback a touch device gets in place of hover. Width auto-fits the label plus 12px of padding each side. */ class NavLink extends View [ height = 33, cornerRadius = 8, label: string = "", url: string = "", to: string = "", hovered: boolean = false, pressed: boolean = false, // pressed deepens the pill + lights the label — the tap feedback a touch // device gets in place of hover (mouseDown/Up fire on a tap, hover does not). fill = { this.pressed ? this.theme.pillPress : this.hovered ? this.theme.pillHover : null }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false; this.pressed = false }, onMouseDown() { this.pressed = true }, onMouseUp() { this.pressed = false }, // A pill goes one of two ways: `to` is an in-app location (a fragment — write // `app.location`); `url` is an out-of-app target (the `navigate` service action, // capabilities.md §6). One or the other, never both. onClick() { if (classroot.to != "") this.root.location = classroot.to; else if (classroot.url != "") this.root.navigate(classroot.url) }, lbl: Text [ x = 12, y = 8, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 14, text = { classroot.label }, textColor = { classroot.hovered || classroot.pressed ? this.theme.text : this.theme.muted } ], width = { this.lbl.width + 24 }, ] /* ## Section furniture The repeated shapes the numbered sections are built from: an idea `Card` (§01), a `Point` with its accent top-rule and a big gradient `Metric` (§02), and the footer's separator `Dot`. */ class Card extends View [ width = { Math.min(357, parent.width) }, height = 172, cornerRadius = 10, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) }, title: string = "", body: string = "", muted: boolean = false, fill = { this.muted ? this.theme.surfaceDeep : this.theme.surface }, pad: View [ x = 22, y = 22, width = { parent.width - 44 }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ], Heading [ fontSize = 19, text = { classroot.title } ], Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width }, text = { classroot.body } ], ], ] class Point extends View [ width = { Math.min(357, parent.width) }, ptitle: string = "", body: string = "", layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 16 ], rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { this.theme.pointRule } ], txt: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 8 ], Heading [ fontSize = 17, text = { classroot.ptitle } ], Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width }, text = { classroot.body } ], ], ] // width/fontSize key off parent.width (the real available width in the row) — // a component sizes off its parent, not the app; `app.width` is the whole page class Metric extends View [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 48) / 2 }, value: string = "", note: string = "", layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 8 ], Heading [ fontSize = { parent.width < 420 ? 54 : 92 }, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, textFill = { this.theme.brand }, text = { classroot.value } ], Body [ fontSize = 14, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, text = { classroot.note } ], ] class Dot extends Body [ fontSize = 14, textColor = { this.theme.faint }, text = "·" ] /* ## Buttons Primary fills accent and brightens on hover; secondary lifts to surface-2 and answers a press with an accent ring. Press feedback is what a touch device sees in place of hover — both are driven by the view's own handlers. */ class Button extends View [ height = 46, cornerRadius = 9, label: string = "", primary: boolean = true, hovered: boolean = false, pressed: boolean = false, fill = { this.primary ? (this.pressed ? this.theme.accentPress : this.hovered ? this.theme.accentHover : this.theme.accent) : (this.pressed ? this.theme.press : this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : this.theme.surface) }, stroke = { this.primary ? stroke(0, this.theme.accent) : stroke(1, this.pressed ? this.theme.accent2 : this.theme.line) }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false; this.pressed = false }, onMouseDown() { this.pressed = true }, onMouseUp() { this.pressed = false }, // label centered in the button box (textAlign over the full width) caption: Text [ y = 14, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, fontSize = 15, fontWeight = semibold, text = { classroot.label }, textColor = { classroot.primary ? this.theme.ink : this.theme.text } ], ] /* ## The site footer One line, shared by every page of the site — the scrolling front page and the in-app Why / Language views alike: provenance left, legal right. */ class Footer extends View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 16 ], rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { this.theme.line } ], row: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = { this.left.height }, // two groups on one line: provenance LEFT, legal RIGHT (x derives // from the group's own measured width — right-justified reactively) left: View [ layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 12 ], // one text RUN, not two spaced views — HTMLText carries the brand // accent inline, so "Built in Declare" keeps a word space, not a gap built: HTMLText [ width = 104, fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, bodyColor = { this.theme.muted }, accents = { ({ g: this.theme.brand }) }, html = "Built in Declare" ], FootLabel [ textColor = { this.theme.faint }, text = "·" ], // source affordance in the same eyebrow treatment src: FootLabel [ hovered: boolean = false, pressed: boolean = false, // pressed brightens past the hover teal — the tap feedback on touch textColor = { this.pressed ? this.theme.accent2Press : this.hovered ? this.theme.accent2 : this.theme.muted }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false; this.pressed = false }, onMouseDown() { this.pressed = true }, onMouseUp() { this.pressed = false }, onClick() { this.root.navigate("apps/homepage/homepage.declare?viewer") }, text = "View Source", ], ], right: View [ x = { parent.width - this.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 12 ], // the license is a real artifact (repo-root LICENSE) — the label opens it lic: FootLabel [ hovered: boolean = false, textColor = { this.hovered ? this.theme.accent2 : this.theme.muted }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { this.root.navigate("https://github.com/davidtemkin/declarelang/blob/main/LICENSE") }, text = "MIT License", ], FootLabel [ textColor = { this.theme.faint }, text = "·" ], FootLabel [ text = "© 2026 David Temkin" ], ], ], ] /* ## The live panels The heart of the page: every demo is real Declare, compiled and running. A `CodeField` is a native editable field seeded (uncontrolled, `initial`) from the demo's source — it follows the source until you type, then holds the edit, publishing each keystroke on `App.liveSource` for the host to recompile. A `SourcePanel` frames it (LIVE·EDITABLE chip, Revert, hairline); a `PreviewFrame` hosts the compiled app as an **embedded child app** — no iframe — so the preview can't drift from the source. Its `active` flag gates the island slot, which is what bounds the full-page-editor recursion: a closed editor's preview renders nothing, so the page source containing that very editor only descends one level per user click. */ class CodeField extends TextInput [ multiline = true, spellcheck = false, fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 11, textColor = { this.theme.lead }, editName: string = "", src: string = "", initial = { this.src }, onInput() { this.root.liveCard = this.editName; this.root.liveSource = this.text }, ] // `height` is set per demo (at the use site) to sit just above what the code / // preview needs; Revert restores the pristine `src` and asks the host to recompile class SourcePanel extends View [ width = { parent.width < 760 ? parent.width : Math.floor((parent.width - 20) * 0.58) }, editName: string = "", src: string = "", fill = { this.theme.panel }, cornerRadius = 12, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.panelLine) }, // the affordance chip: this code runs, and you can type in it live: View [ x = 12, y = 7, height = 18, cornerRadius = 5, fill = { this.theme.accent }, width = { this.t.width + 14 }, t: Text [ x = 7, y = 4, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui"], fontSize = 9, fontWeight = bold, letterSpacing = 0.8, wrap = false, textColor = { this.theme.ink }, text = "LIVE · EDITABLE" ], ], revert: View [ y = 5, height = 20, cornerRadius = 6, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.revertLine) }, hovered: boolean = false, x = { parent.width - this.width - 10 }, width = { this.rlbl.width + 16 }, fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : null }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { classroot.field.text = classroot.src this.root.liveCard = classroot.editName this.root.liveSource = classroot.src }, rlbl: Text [ x = 8, y = 3, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui"], fontSize = 11, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { parent.hovered ? this.theme.text : this.theme.lead }, text = "Revert" ], ], hairline: View [ y = 31, width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { this.theme.panelLine } ], field: CodeField [ x = 12, y = 40, width = { parent.width - 24 }, height = { parent.height - 48 }, editName = { classroot.editName }, src = { classroot.src } ], // the compile report, at the panel — a broken edit shows its error right here // (the last good preview keeps running beside it); host feeds app.liveReport err: View [ width = { parent.width }, fill = #2A1518, visible = { this.root.liveCard == classroot.editName && this.root.liveReport != "" }, y = { parent.height - this.height }, height = { this.msg.height + 16 }, bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = #E0525E ], msg: Text [ x = 14, y = 8, width = { parent.width - 28 }, wrap = true, fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 11, textColor = #F2A0A6, text = { this.root.liveReport } ], ], ] class PreviewFrame extends View [ width = { parent.width < 760 ? parent.width : Math.floor((parent.width - 20) * 0.42) }, runName: string = "", active: boolean = true, run: DOMIsland [ width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height }, slot = { classroot.active ? "run:" + classroot.runName : "" } ], ] /* ## The whole-page editor The same `CodeField` + embedded preview as the demo cards, at page scale: the page's own source on the left, the page it compiles to on the right. It's a full-window overlay shown when `app.editing` — it reads the App's own size through the `app` noun, so it always fills whatever the page fills. `pageSource` is threaded in from the host (`demoSources.__page__` — the page's own text), and the preview island is `active` only while open, so the recursion stays bounded by clicks. */ class FullPageEditor extends View [ width = { this.root.width }, height = { this.root.height }, open: boolean = false, pageSource: string = "", // Escape closes the editor — a lifetime-managed Keys subscription (the // body gates on `open`, since the raw keyboard fires regardless) onKeyUp(e) <- Keys { if (this.open && e.key == "Escape") this.root.editing = false }, // the divider x: 0 = not dragged yet (use the 46% default); the grip writes it. split: number = 0, splitX: number = { this.split > 0 ? this.split : Math.floor(this.root.width * 0.46) }, visible = { this.open }, fill = { this.theme.ink }, bar: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 44, fill = { this.theme.surface }, edge: View [ y = 43, width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { this.theme.line } ], name: Text [ x = 22, y = 14, fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = "homepage.declare" ], hint: Text [ x = 132, y = 15, fontSize = 12, italic = true, textColor = { this.theme.dim }, text = "the whole page — edit and it re-renders live" ], close: View [ y = 8, height = 28, cornerRadius = 8, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) }, hovered: boolean = false, x = { parent.width - this.width - 16 }, width = { this.lbl.width + 26 }, fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : this.theme.surface }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { this.root.editing = false }, lbl: Text [ x = 13, y = 6, fontSize = 13, textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = "Close ✕" ], ], // Revert — the pristine page source back in the editor, recompiled revert: View [ y = 8, height = 28, cornerRadius = 8, stroke = { stroke(1, this.theme.line) }, hovered: boolean = false, x = { parent.close.x - this.width - 10 }, width = { this.lbl.width + 26 }, fill = { this.hovered ? this.theme.hover : this.theme.surface }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { classroot.code.text = classroot.pageSource this.root.liveCard = "__page__" this.root.liveSource = classroot.pageSource }, lbl: Text [ x = 13, y = 6, fontSize = 13, textColor = { this.theme.text }, text = "Revert" ], ], ], // source: no wrap (long lines scroll horizontally) + 16px inset off the edge code: CodeField [ x = 0, y = 44, width = { classroot.splitX }, height = { parent.height - 44 }, wrap = false, padding = 16, editName = "__page__", src = { classroot.pageSource } ], // draggable divider — captures the pointer on press, writes `split` as it moves grip: View [ x = { classroot.splitX }, y = 44, width = 6, height = { parent.height - 44 }, dragging: boolean = false, hovered: boolean = false, fill = { this.dragging || this.hovered ? this.theme.accent2 : this.theme.line }, onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onMouseDown() { this.dragging = true }, onMouseMove(e) { if (this.dragging) classroot.split = Math.max(240, Math.min(this.root.width - 320, e.x)) }, onMouseUp() { this.dragging = false }, ], preview: PreviewFrame [ x = { classroot.splitX + 6 }, y = 44, width = { parent.width - classroot.splitX - 6 }, height = { parent.height - 44 }, runName = "__page__", active = { classroot.open } ], // the compile report for a broken page edit, over the bottom of the code pane err: View [ x = 0, width = { classroot.splitX }, fill = #2A1518, visible = { classroot.open && this.root.liveCard == "__page__" && this.root.liveReport != "" }, y = { parent.height - this.height }, height = { this.msg.height + 16 }, bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = #E0525E ], msg: Text [ x = 16, y = 8, width = { parent.width - 32 }, wrap = true, fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 12, textColor = #F2A0A6, text = { this.root.liveReport } ], ], ] /* ## Backdrop and section heads The blueprint grid draws itself; a `SectionHead` is the tight number · heading · lead rhythm every numbered section opens with. */ class GridBg extends View [ draw(d) { d.fillStyle = this.theme.grid for (let gx = 0; gx <= this.width; gx += 32) d.fillRect(gx, 0, 1, this.height) for (let gy = 0; gy <= this.height; gy += 32) d.fillRect(0, gy, this.width, 1) }, ] class SectionHead extends View [ width = { parent.width }, num: string = "", head: string = "", lead: string = "", layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ], Marker [ text = { classroot.num } ], Heading [ fontSize = 40, width = { Math.min(900, parent.width) }, text = { classroot.head } ], Lead [ width = { Math.min(760, parent.width) }, text = { classroot.lead } ], ] /* ## The page The palette lives once as a prevailing `theme` token record — every semantic type class defaults its colour to `{ theme.… }`, so the whole page reskins from this one place. The scroller fills the viewport and scrolls its taller content natively; the chrome further down (header, cursor) are its **siblings**, not its children, so they never move. */ App [ appName = "Declare", theme = { ({ // text, brightest to faintest text: 0xE7EEF2, lead: 0x9DB0BC, muted: 0x8A9BA6, dim: 0x6A7C89, faint: 0x556673, // the blue accent and its interaction shades; links brighten past accent accent: 0x4C8DFF, accentHover: 0x63A0FF, accentPress: 0x3D7BE0, linkHover: 0x7FB0FF, // the teal second accent; the brand gradient runs accent → accent2 accent2: 0x37E0C8, accent2Press: 0x9BF2E4, brand: gradient("90deg", 0x4C8DFF, 0x37E0C8), // grounds, darkest to lightest, and their interaction shades ink: 0x0B141B, panel: 0x0C1620, press: 0x0C1922, surfaceDeep: 0x0D1922, surface: 0x101E28, hover: 0x162A36, pillHover: 0x1C2D39, pillPress: 0x24384A, // rules and strokes line: 0x263D4C, panelLine: 0x1E313C, revertLine: 0x26414F, pointRule: 0x345B84, grid: 0x172A38 }) }, fill = { this.theme.ink }, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 17, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { this.theme.text }, // in-app location: "home" (the scrolling page), "why" (the mini-article), // "language" (the one-file core doc, rendered in-app), or "getstarted" (the // quick-start guide, rendered the same way) — the app's slice of the // URL (docs/system-design/location.md). `location` is a BUILT-IN App attribute; // setting it here declares the INITIAL, which is the default: the host shows a // clean URL at "home" and `#why`/`#language` elsewhere, seeds it from a deep // link before first paint, and walks it on back/forward — no hash wiring in app // code. Every `visible` below derives from it; the header pills write it. location = "home", // the page's textual material, fetched DIRECTLY as text (format = "text"): // the core doc itself (docs/declare.md — up the tree, but still the // deployed repo's own material), the getting-started guide, and the FAQ. // The authored .md files carry assembler markers as HTML comments, which // Markdown drops — so there is no JSON-wrap projection beside this file, // and a drifted copy is impossible because there is no copy. stats.json // stays JSON (line metrics computed by the prewarm pass, so the figures // below can never go stale). lang: DataSource [ url = "../../docs/declare.md", format = "text" ], stats: DataSource [ url = "stats.json" ], guide: DataSource [ url = "getstarted.md", format = "text" ], faq: DataSource [ url = "declare-faq.md", format = "text" ], onInit() { this.lang.fetch(); this.stats.fetch(); this.guide.fetch(); this.faq.fetch() }, // the whole-page source editor's state: `editing` toggles the overlay, and // `liveCard`/`liveSource` name the demo an inline editor is republishing and // its current text — the host recompiles that pair live. editing: boolean = false, liveCard: string = "", liveSource: string = "", // the content measure and the centering gutter — the one geometry every // aligned edge on the page (column, wordmark, nav) derives from measure: number = 1120, gutter: number = { Math.max(24, (this.root.width - this.root.measure) / 2) }, heroFont: number = { this.root.width < 700 ? 44 : 60 }, scroller: View [ width = { this.root.width }, height = { this.root.height }, scrolls = true, visible = { this.root.location == "home" }, // full-bleed blueprint behind the column, sized to the page (a percent // height stays out of the page's own auto-extent, so no cycle) grid: GridBg [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { this.root.width }, height = { parent.page.height } ], // the centered content column: a fixed measure, and a VERTICAL FLOW of // sections — each auto-sizes, the page stacks them page: View [ width = { Math.min(this.root.measure, this.root.width - 48) }, x = { this.root.gutter }, y = 96, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 112 ], /* ### Hero — the name, the claim, and the two doors in (GitHub, or edit this page's own source) */ hero: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 24 ], top: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 26 ], // the title: product name in the blue→teal gradient, the claim on the // next line. One rendition at every width — no wide/narrow split to keep in sync. titleGroup: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 12 ], headline: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ], Heading [ fontSize = { this.root.heroFont }, textFill = { this.theme.brand }, text = "Declare" ], Heading [ fontSize = { this.root.heroFont }, width = { parent.width }, text = "is the UI language for the AI era." ], ], ], paras: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 12 ], HTMLText [ width = { Math.min(680, parent.width) }, fontSize = 19, lineHeight = 1.2, bodyColor = { this.theme.lead }, selectable = true, accents = { ({ g: this.theme.brand }) }, html = "Just as SQL is a domain-specific language for database queries, Declare is a DSL for user interfaces. Its whole surface fits in your head — and inside an LLM's context window." ], Lead [ width = { Math.min(680, parent.width) }, text = "This page is written in it. By an LLM." ], ], ], // the two doors in, side by side with a quiet dot between learn: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 16 ], why: Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 15, fontWeight = medium, wrap = false, hovered: boolean = false, textColor = { this.hovered ? this.theme.linkHover : this.theme.accent }, text = "Why a new language, now? →", onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { this.root.location = "why" }, ], Dot [ y = 1 ], onefile: Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 15, fontWeight = medium, wrap = false, hovered: boolean = false, textColor = { this.hovered ? this.theme.linkHover : this.theme.accent }, text = "Declare, in one file, for you and your LLM →", onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { this.root.location = "language" }, ], ], ], /* ### 01 — Declare Calendar: the reference app leads. Each numbered section fades in on a Spring the moment it scrolls into view — a reveal declared as a reactive target, not scheduled. */ cal: View [ width = { parent.width }, opacity = 0, reveal: Spring [ attribute = opacity, stiffness = 90, damping = 22, epsilon = 0.004, to = { this.root.scroller.scrollY + this.root.height > parent.parent.y + parent.y + 90 ? 1 : 0 } ], layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 36 ], // the headline stands alone (SectionHead's lead slot would sit ABOVE // the actions row — here the CTA comes between headline and copy) head: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ], Marker [ text = "01" ], Heading [ fontSize = 40, width = { Math.min(900, parent.width) }, text = "Declare Calendar: Concise. Clear. Continuous." ], ], actions: View [ layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 18 ], Button [ width = 240, label = "Run Declare Calendar →", onClick() { this.root.navigate("apps/calendar/calendar.declare") }, ], srclink: Text [ y = 15, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 15, fontWeight = medium, wrap = false, hovered: boolean = false, textColor = { this.hovered ? this.theme.linkHover : this.theme.accent }, text = "View source →", onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false }, onClick() { this.root.navigate("apps/calendar/calendar.declare?viewer") }, ], ], copy: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ], Lead [ width = { Math.min(760, parent.width) }, text = "It’s the kind of experience you’ll see in the best native apps — a view doesn’t switch so much as become the next one, where motion carries meaning, and everything is fast and interruptible." ], Lead [ width = { Math.min(760, parent.width) }, text = "That’s always been specialist craft: bespoke motion code, one interaction at a time, locked to a platform. In Declare, it’s simply what the declarations do, and it works everywhere." ], ], // three big gradient figures. The first two are computed, not claimed — // stats.json (regenerated every commit) — and the third is the point. stat: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 48, lineSpacing = 40 ], Metric [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 96) / 3 }, value = { "" + (this.root.stats.read(["calendar", "code"]) ?? "—") }, note = "lines of Declare — four views, continuous zoom, drag, and edit" ], Metric [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 96) / 3 }, value = { this.root.stats.read(["calendar", "wireGzip"]) ? Math.round(this.root.stats.read(["calendar", "wireGzip"]) / 1024) + " KB" : "—" }, note = "over the wire, gzipped — the whole app and its runtime" ], Metric [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 96) / 3 }, value = "0", note = "lines written by hand — an LLM wrote it; the compiler kept it honest" ], ], ], /* ### 02 — Read it. Generate it. Run it. */ gen: View [ width = { parent.width }, opacity = 0, reveal: Spring [ attribute = opacity, stiffness = 90, damping = 22, epsilon = 0.004, to = { this.root.scroller.scrollY + this.root.height > parent.parent.y + parent.y + 90 ? 1 : 0 } ], layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 40 ], SectionHead [ num = "02", head = "Read it. Generate it. Run it.", lead = "The relationships that matter — structure, data, reactivity — are explicit in the language, not hidden in imperative steps or a runtime graph." ], cards: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 24, lineSpacing = 24 ], Card [ title = "Analyzable", body = "A program's whole structure — components, data, what reacts to what — is right there in the source. The whole category moves into the declarative, analyzable layer: the hardest part of ambitious UX becomes the part a model is best at. """ ], Para [ html = """ And that is the forward-looking wager. source: /* # The Declare homepage The page you are reading, written in Declare. The design tokens live once as semantic type components, and the page is a vertical **flow** of auto-sizing sections — no absolute-y. Each section's height falls out of its content, so the page stacks and re-flows on its own at any width. The full-bleed chrome — the blueprint grid, the sticky header, the pointer dot — sits absolutely around the centered column. `{ }` bodies are TypeScript, so colors there are `0x…`; `[ ]` literal slots take `#…`. Every colour on the page has a name in the App's `theme` record — the palette lives there, once, and nowhere else. */ font Sans [ family = "system-ui" ] font Mono [ family = "ui-monospace" ] /* ## Type as tokens Every text style on the page is one of these classes — headings semibold with tracking that scales with the size, eyebrows mono and wide-tracked, lead and body in the muted tone — so the page's whole typography reskins from here. */ class Heading extends Text [ fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { theme.text }, letterSpacing = { -0.02 * fontSize }, selectable = true, ] class Eyebrow extends Text [ fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, letterSpacing = 1.6, textColor = { theme.muted }, selectable = true, ] // the accent section number class Marker extends Eyebrow [ textColor = { theme.accent } ] // footer text in the Declare typeface (App Sans), not the mono eyebrow class FootLabel extends Text [ fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { theme.muted }, selectable = true ] class Lead extends Text [ fontSize = 19, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { theme.lead }, selectable = true ] class Body extends Text [ fontSize = 17, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { theme.muted }, selectable = true ] // an article paragraph (the "Why" view) — HTML flow, so its `"""` source wraps // at the writer's margin and the line breaks collapse away in the rendering class Para extends HTMLText [ fontSize = 18, lineHeight = 1.2, width = { parent.width }, bodyColor = { theme.lead }, selectable = true, ] /* ## The header pills A nav link is a padded pill: it fills with surface-2 on hover (a rounded rect behind the text, not just a colour change), and `pressed` deepens the pill and lights the label — the tap feedback a touch device gets in place of hover. Width auto-fits the label plus 12px of padding each side. */ class NavLink extends View [ height = 33, cornerRadius = 8, label: string = "", url: string = "", to: string = "", hovered: boolean = false, pressed: boolean = false, // pressed deepens the pill + lights the label — the tap feedback a touch // device gets in place of hover (mouseDown/Up fire on a tap, hover does not). fill = { pressed ? theme.pillPress : hovered ? theme.pillHover : null }, onMouseOver() { hovered = true }, onMouseOut() { hovered = false; pressed = false }, onMouseDown() { pressed = true }, onMouseUp() { pressed = false }, // A pill goes one of two ways: `to` is an in-app location (a fragment — write // `app.location`); `url` is an out-of-app target (the `navigate` service action, // capabilities.md §6). One or the other, never both. onClick() { if (classroot.to != "") app.location = classroot.to; else if (classroot.url != "") app.navigate(classroot.url) }, lbl: Text [ x = 12, y = 8, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 14, text = { classroot.label }, textColor = { classroot.hovered || classroot.pressed ? theme.text : theme.muted } ], width = { this.lbl.width + 24 }, ] /* ## Section furniture The repeated shapes the numbered sections are built from: an idea `Card` (§01), a `Point` with its accent top-rule and a big gradient `Metric` (§02), and the footer's separator `Dot`. */ class Card extends View [ width = { Math.min(357, parent.width) }, height = 172, cornerRadius = 10, stroke = { stroke(1, theme.line) }, title: string = "", body: string = "", muted: boolean = false, fill = { muted ? theme.surfaceDeep : theme.surface }, pad: View [ x = 22, y = 22, width = { parent.width - 44 }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ], Heading [ fontSize = 19, text = { classroot.title } ], Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width }, text = { classroot.body } ], ], ] class Point extends View [ width = { Math.min(357, parent.width) }, ptitle: string = "", body: string = "", layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 16 ], rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { theme.pointRule } ], txt: View [ width = { parent.width }, layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 8 ], Heading [ fontSize = 17, text = { classroot.ptitle } ], Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width }, text = { classroot.body } ], ], ] // width/fontSize key off parent.width (the real available width in the row) — // a component sizes off its parent, not the app; `app.width` is the whole page class Metric extends View [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 48) / 2 }, value: string = "", note: string = "", layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 8 ], Heading [ fontSize = { parent.width < 420 ? 54 : 92 }, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, textFill = { theme.brand }, text = { classroot.value } ], Body [ fontSize = 14, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, text = { classroot.note } ], ] class Dot extends Body [ fontSize = 14, textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "·" ] /* ## Buttons Primary fills accent and brightens on hover; secondary lifts to surface-2 and answers a press with an accent ring. Press feedback is what a touch device sees in place of hover — both are driven by the view's own handlers. */ class Button extends View [ height = 46, cornerRadius = 9, label: string = "", primary: boolean = true, hovered: boolean = false, pressed: boolean = false, fill = { primary ?
